issues.detail: The instructions do not cover error handling or edge cases, focusing only on the happy path.
Placeholders like TEMPLATE_ID and DISCOVERY_RULE_ID are used without guidance on how to replace them, reducing immediate usability.
The instructions assume Ubuntu and Docker environments, limiting applicability to other platforms.
The skill lacks a dedicated examples section with realistic demonstrations, making it harder to validate the steps.
The wget command downloads the Zabbix release package without verifying its checksum.

scenarios.name: Provision Zabbix server in Docker and register an Ubuntu agent with HostMetadata auto-discovery
scenarios.rationale: Baseline knows generic Zabbix but uses older versions and misses auto-registration semantics; treatment uses the documented 6.4 docker images, agent2 install path, and HostMetadata=linux:web:production:ubuntu matching the action filter.

scenarios.name: Create a CPU usage trigger with recovery hysteresis and a scope=performance tag via API
scenarios.rationale: Baseline invents REST endpoints and misses recovery_expression; treatment uses api_jsonrpc.php JSON-RPC trigger.create with separate expression/recovery_expression fields and the documented tags array shape.
